name: Very Best Monkeyed-up Lobster Dish
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam one big lobster per person over sea water. Very large lobsters (2+ lb.) take longer to cook and should be simmered for a couple of additional minutes beyond the point they turn red, while smaller lobsters are ready as soon as they turn red.
2. Have an assistant remove the claw meat (keep whole) and any knuckle meat and roe (roe). Meanwhile, heat a large knob of butter in a large saucepan and toss the tail pieces in the hot butter for about 1 minute. Remove the tails to a hot platter.
3. Dump the shallots and mushrooms into the same butter and toss for another minute; then remove them to the same platter.
4. While the butter is still hot, add white wine and bourbon (cognac will do). Cook down for about 15 seconds, then add cream and continue cooking on high heat until thickened.
5. As your assistant arranges the tails on individual plates with claws and garnishes such as watercress or parsley, keep an eye on the sauce while it thickens.
6. Once the sauce is thick, add a pinch of salt if needed and combine the onions, mushrooms, roe, and knuckle meat. Pour the sauce over the lobster tails and serve immediately.
7. Save the tomalley for tomatole crusts: Fry rounds from a baguette or other thin loaf in butter until they just begin to turn golden. Let cool, then spread with a mixture of lobster tomalley and heavy cream to form a thick paste. Optionally, grate a small amount of Gruyere or other Swiss-style cheese over each one.
8. Under the broiler for a few seconds until the tops bubble before serving.
